scope.target: HANDOFF-001 provider-native attribution preservation and execution provenance planning
constraints: Preserve provider-native Git attribution and signatures
Separate commit attribution from execution provenance
Label runtime-reported, provider-reported, user-declared, and unavailable provenance distinctly
Require real provider-generated fixtures before policy or validator implementation

steps: Inspect current policy and validator behavior
Research official Copilot CLI and cloud-agent attribution behavior
Inspect official Claude Code documentation without inferring an undocumented attribution contract
Confirm current Codex model and reasoning effort from runtime request metadata
Add preservation, evidence-union, provenance-source, and fixture gates to HANDOFF-001
Validate and close the planning workflow
execution.last_completed_step: Updated and validated HANDOFF-001 plus the provider-native attribution research record.
execution.next_action: None in this workflow; implement only through the v0.5.0 HANDOFF-001 workflow after provider fixtures are available.

results.summary: Made provider-native attribution preservation, supplemental execution provenance, provenance-source labeling, and real-provider golden fixtures explicit HANDOFF-001 gates.
results.residual_risk: The current commit policy and validator remain unchanged and can reject provider-native attribution shapes; HANDOFF-001 implementation must close that gap after real provider fixtures are captured. The first bare-bash quick-gate attempt routed to unconfigured WSL and exited before checks; the explicit Git for Windows Bash rerun passed.
